message:
http/h1: parse unbracketed IPv6 hosts using scheme default port

`ParsedUrl::parse` previously used a single `authority.rfind(':')` to
split the host/port pair, which is correct for `example.com:8080`,
correct for `[::1]:8080` (bracketed IPv6), but wrong for an
unbracketed IPv6 literal like `http://::1/healthz`. In that case
`rfind(':')` returned the offset of the last colon inside the
address itself, and the parser then tried to interpret `1` as a port
and the preceding `::` as the host, which breaks any caller that
hands the parser a health-check URL built from a raw IPv6 address
without brackets.

Add a branch before the `rfind(':')` port split: if the authority is
not wrapped in `[...]` *and* contains more than one colon, treat it
as an unbracketed IPv6 literal and return `(authority, default_port)`
where `default_port` is 80 for `Scheme::Http` and 443 for
`Scheme::Https`. This matches the observable behavior of other HTTP
clients and keeps the existing bracketed and single-colon code paths
untouched.

Add the regression test `parse_url_handles_unbracketed_ipv6_host` to
pin `http://::1/healthz` -> `host = "::1"`, `port = 80`, so a future
refactor of the authority parser cannot silently regress the case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
